title: Usure Nee Lyrics Mahabalipuram Song Lyrics
stanzas:
Mahabalipuram Usure Nee Song Lyrics
Singers: Imman, Shakthisree
Music: K
Lyrics:Yugabharathi,

Usure nee kalangadhey
oru podhum varundhadhey
iravodum pagalodum
thuniyaaven thugalaadhe.

Usure nee thonai aanaa
endha sogam thoraththaadhe
kanavodum nenavodum
kadha pesu vilagadhey

Unadhu thozhil saaindhu
uranga pidikkudhey.
unadhu mugathai paarthu
vizhikka pidikkudhey

Usure nee kalangadhey
oru podhum varundhadhey
iravodum pagalodum
thuniyaaven thugalaaiven

Unake pirandhen
ena naan unardhaen.
edhayum, athanaal
tharavum thuniyuren.

Nodiyum koduthaal
minidam tharugiren.
unai nee koduthaal
tharave maattaen
en kannae.

Usure nee kalangadhey
oru podhum varundhadhey
kanavodum nenavodum
kadha pesu vilagadhey

Irulil vilakka
inaitha velicham nee
uyire unakke
tharuven naaney
en anbae.

Usure nee kalangadhey
oru podhum varundhadhey
iravodum pagalodum
thuniyaaven thugalaadhe.

Usure nee thonai aanaa
endha sogam thoraththaadhe
kanavodum nenavodum
kadha pesu vilagadhey

Unadhu thozhil saaindhu
uranga pidikkudhey.
unadhu mugathai paarthu
vizhikka pidikkudhey

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song 'Usure Nee'?

'Usure Nee' conveys feelings of love and emotional connection, expressing the beauty of companionship.

How does this song fit into the plot of the movie Mahabalipuram?

The song enhances the romantic moments in Mahabalipuram, highlighting the emotional bond between the characters.

Who is the singer and music director of this song?

The song is sung by D Imman and composed by KrishnaKumar.

When was 'Usure Nee' released?

'Usure Nee' was released on March 11, 2015.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In 'Usure Nee', the protagonist expresses deep emotions of love and longing, making it a poignant addition to the film *Mahabalipuram*. The song's soothing melody and heartfelt lyrics create an atmosphere of romance, resonating with anyone who has experienced love. It beautifully encapsulates the essence of companionship, making it perfect for intimate occasions. The collaboration between D Imman's vocals and KrishnaKumar's composition elevates the emotional depth of the song.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Usure Nee from Mahabalipuram is a captivating melody that beautifully captures the essence of love and longing. With D Imman's soulful vocals and KrishnaKumar's enchanting composition, this song resonates deeply with listeners. Experience the magic of this romantic track that perfectly complements the film's narrative.
stanzaMeanings: You are not lost; it's enough to feel troubled. Day and night, don't let worries disturb you.
You are my companion, so don't show any sadness. Dreams and thoughts shouldn't speak out loud.
Your work is making me sleepy. Looking at your face makes me feel drowsy.
You are not lost; it's enough to feel troubled. Day and night, I won't let worries disturb me.
I was born for you, and I understand you. Because of that, I also feel tired.
When I give you my gaze, I will give you light. If you give yourself to me, I won't let go, my dear.
You are not lost; it's enough to feel troubled. Dreams and thoughts shouldn't speak out loud.
I have grown up crying for you. I only smile when I think of you.
In the darkness, you are the light that I will give to you, my love.
You are not lost; it's enough to feel troubled. Day and night, don't let worries disturb you.
You are my companion, so don't show any sadness. Dreams and thoughts shouldn't speak out loud.
Your work is making me sleepy. Looking at your face makes me feel drowsy.
